hadith_primary.translation_en: Five animals are fāsiq (harmful) and may be killed even in the Haram: the crow, the kite, the scorpion, the rat, and the biting dog.

eschatology.qiyamah_note: All of Allah's creation will be gathered on Qiyamah. Scorpions are communities like us (Qur'an 6:38). Kill only when necessary and do not transgress the bounds of necessity.
eschatology.ecological_note: Scorpions are natural controllers of insect populations. Targeted elimination around the home is sufficient; do not seek mass extermination of non-threatening populations.

affected_regions: Uttar Pradesh
Bihar
Madhya Pradesh becoming increasingly endemic
climate_future.projection: By 2050, active season projected to extend 2–3 additional months in northern India; incidence in urban areas likely to rise with informal housing construction

faq.q: Can I kill a scorpion during Ramadan or Ihram?
faq.a: Yes. The scorpion is explicitly named as a Fāsiq creature in Sahih Bukhari; killing it is permissible at all times, including during Ihram in the Haram.

faq.q: What if I find one in my shoe?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ instructed us to shake our shoes before wearing them. If found, crush it swiftly with a hard object.

faq.q: Is anti-venom available in India?
faq.a: Yes, at government hospitals, especially in Maharashtra, Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Andhra Pradesh. Scorpion anti-venom is government-supplied and free.

faq.q: Do scorpions come out in winter?
faq.a: They are less active below 15°C but do not fully hibernate. Check dark corners and stored clothing year-round.

faq.q: Is it Haram to keep a scorpion as a pet?
faq.a: The majority of scholars hold that keeping harmful creatures without need is makruh (disliked). If it poses a risk to household members, it must be removed.

symptoms: Silent envenomation with delayed respiratory failure (Krait)
health.fatality_india: ~58,000 deaths/year — India accounts for ~50% of global snakebite deaths
first_aid: Immobilise the bitten limb (splint if possible)
Remove jewellery near bite site
Do NOT apply tourniquet, slash wound, or suck venom
Rush to hospital immediately; anti-venom is effective

islamic_ruling.status: Al-Hayyah al-Mu'dhi — Dangerous snake: Permissible and encouraged to kill

hadith_primary.source: Sahih al-Bukhari 3297; Sahih Muslim 2233
hadith_primary.translation_en: Kill the snakes; and kill the two-striped snake and the short-tailed snake, for they cause blindness and miscarriage.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: Iqtulu al-hayyati waqtulu dha't-tufyatayni wa'l-abtara fa-innahuma yatmisani al-basara wa-yusqitani al-habal.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Dangerous snakes must be killed; non-dangerous desert snakes require a warning before killing
fiqh_ruling.shafii: All harmful snakes permissible to kill; caution needed with jinn-inhabited house snakes
fiqh_ruling.hanbali: Permissible; striking once is recommended before killing
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Killing dangerous snakes is an obligation when they pose a threat

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: Strike swiftly with a stick or heavy object. Warn a snake in the house three days before killing (for non-aggressive house snake — Hadith precaution). Do not burn.

duas.transliteration: A'udhu bi kalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq

prophetic_practice: Recite evening adhkar before sleeping
Keep home clean and free of rodents (which attract snakes)
Close doors and windows at dusk
Do not walk barefoot in the dark or in tall grass

Keep grass short and remove brush/woodpiles
Seal gaps under doors, around pipes, and in foundations
Remove debris such as tin sheets, bricks, and compost heaps near the home

Use solid door sweeps
Ensure poultry coops are snake-proof (snakes hunt eggs and chicks)
Install snake-proof fencing for children's play areas if in high-risk zones

Perimeter spray of naphthalene balls as a deterrent (limited effectiveness)
For a snake that has entered the home: open doors and allow it to exit; do not corner it
prohibited_methods: Starving a captured snake for days before killing

eschatology.qiyamah_note: Snakes are among Allah's creation. The Prophet ﷺ distinguished between harmful attacking snakes and house serpents. Even when killing is necessary, do so swiftly and do not transgress.
eschatology.ecological_note: Snakes are apex rodent controllers. Non-venomous and non-threatening species should be relocated, not killed.

climate_future.trend: Range shifts and altered hibernation patterns due to climate warming
affected_regions: Higher altitude regions of Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand seeing snake activity at higher elevations
Urban heat islands drawing snakes into cities year-round
climate_future.projection: Extended active season; monsoon expansion may push snakes into new residential areas

faq.q: Should I warn a house snake before killing it?
faq.a: Yes — for a non-aggressive snake that has made its home in your house, scholars recommend warning it three times over three days. If it persists, it may be killed.

faq.q: Is it permissible to kill a non-venomous snake?
faq.a: If it is not posing a threat, scholars differ. Many recommend relocation over killing for non-venomous species. If it is threatening, killing is permissible.

faq.q: What is the anti-venom situation in India?
faq.a: Polyvalent anti-venom covers India's Big Four (Cobra, Krait, Russell's Viper, Saw-scaled Viper). It is free at government hospitals. Time is critical — get there within 2 hours.

faq.q: Can I kill a snake during Ihram?
faq.a: Yes. Dangerous snakes may be killed even during Ihram, according to the majority of scholars.

faq.q: Is snake charming / keeping a snake haram?
faq.a: Keeping venomous snakes without need is generally forbidden (haram) due to the danger it poses to oneself and others.

symptoms: Sharp burning pain at sting site
Anaphylaxis: throat swelling, difficulty breathing, low blood pressure
health.fatality_india: ~50–100 deaths/year; mass attack fatalities increasing with deforestation
first_aid: Move away from nest immediately
Remove stingers if visible (wasps can sting multiple times)
Apply cold compress
Take antihistamine; for anaphylaxis — inject epinephrine and call 108 immediately

islamic_ruling.status: Mu'dhī — Harmful insect; permissible to kill when threatening

hadith_primary.source: Sunan Abu Dawud 5268; classified as Hasan
hadith_primary.translation_en: General principle: harmful insects that attack humans may be killed. The Zunbūr (wasp/hornet) is classified under creatures whose harm warrants killing.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: La darara wa la dirar.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Permissible to kill when aggressive; disliked to destroy nests unnecessarily
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Permissible when it poses a direct threat
fiqh_ruling.hanbali: Permissible; distinction between colony destruction and individual defence
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Permissible when harm is clear and present

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: Do not burn the nest — use insecticide spray at night when wasps are dormant. Swift death is required.

duas.transliteration: A'udhu bi kalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
duas.translation_en: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.

prophetic_practice: Keep food and sweet drinks covered outdoors
Be cautious near fruit trees and flower beds
Wear light colours when working outdoors (wasps are attracted to dark colours)

Remove fallen fruit from the ground
Keep rubbish bins tightly sealed
Avoid leaving sweet drinks uncovered outdoors
physical_barriers: Install insect mesh over ventilation openings
Use deterrent sprays of peppermint oil around common nesting sites
Place wasp decoy nests (wasps are territorial and avoid other colonies)

Professional pest control for large or inaccessible nests

Insecticide dust blown into wall cavities
prohibited_methods: Poking a live nest repeatedly without protective gear (endangers neighbours)

eschatology.qiyamah_note: Wasps are among the communities of Allah's creation. Kill only when they pose a threat; do not destroy entire colonies out of fear alone.
eschatology.ecological_note: Wasps are beneficial predators that control caterpillar and insect populations. Remove nests only when they are in direct contact with human living areas.

climate_future.trend: Warmer winters allow larger colony survival and earlier spring activity
affected_regions: Northern India seeing hornet activity in months previously too cold
Urban heat islands extending foraging seasons
climate_future.projection: Giant Asian hornet (Vespa mandarinia) range may expand into northeastern India by 2040

faq.q: Is it permissible to destroy a wasp nest?
faq.a: Yes, if it poses a risk to household members. Use insecticide at night when wasps are inactive rather than fire.

faq.q: What should I do if multiple wasps attack me?
faq.a: Run indoors or into a vehicle immediately. Do NOT swat — it releases pheromones that attract more wasps. Jump into water if available.

faq.q: Can I keep a wasps' nest if it's not near my home?
faq.a: Yes. If the nest is in a tree far from the house, leave it alone — wasps provide significant ecological benefit.

faq.q: Are hornets and wasps the same ruling in Islam?
faq.a: Scholars treat all attacking stinging insects under the same general ruling of permissibility to repel and kill when they cause harm.

faq.q: Is the bee different from the wasp?
faq.a: Yes. Bees are explicitly protected in Islamic tradition (honey bee / nahlah). The ruling on aggressive wasps is different from bees.

first_aid: Wash wound with soap and water

Seek medical care if swelling spreads or child is bitten

islamic_ruling.status: Mu'dhī — Harmful creature; permissible to kill

eschatology.ecological_note: Centipedes are natural predators of cockroaches and other pests. In non-living areas (gardens, forests), they provide beneficial pest control.

New northern range expansion in warmer winters

faq.q: Is a centipede bite dangerous?
faq.a: It is extremely painful but rarely dangerous for healthy adults. Children and elderly people should seek medical attention.

faq.q: Why do centipedes come inside the house?
faq.a: They seek moisture and prey. Keep bathrooms dry and seal gaps to prevent entry.

faq.q: Can centipedes climb walls?
faq.a: Yes — they are excellent climbers. Check ceiling corners and behind furniture.

faq.q: Is it permissible to kill them?
faq.a: Yes. They are classified as harmful (mu'dhī) creatures and may be killed when found in living spaces.

faq.q: Do centipedes lay eggs in homes?
faq.a: Rarely. They prefer to lay eggs in damp soil outdoors. Reducing indoor moisture disrupts their lifecycle.

first_aid: Wash wound with soap and water

fiqh_ruling.maliki: Permissible when harm is established

eschatology.qiyamah_note: The Prophet ﷺ was protected by the web of a non-venomous spider in the cave of Thawr. The spider is honoured in Islamic memory. Be careful not to harm beneficial spiders when targeting the harmful.

climate_future.trend: Range expansion of tropical venomous species into new Indian states

faq.q: How do I identify a venomous spider?
faq.a: Venomous Indian spiders include the Indian ornamental tarantula (large, black/grey with patterns) and the redback widow (black with red stripe). Most small brown/grey house spiders are harmless.

faq.q: Is the spider that spun a web over the Prophet's cave the same?
faq.a: That was a non-venomous common spider. It is honoured and should not be harmed. Killing rule applies only to confirmed venomous species.

faq.q: What if I cannot tell if it is venomous?
faq.a: Use a glass to capture and relocate it outdoors. When in doubt, do not kill.

faq.q: Are tarantulas in India dangerous?
faq.a: Indian ornamental tarantulas have medically significant venom and should not be handled. Keep away from children and pets.

faq.q: Can I keep a tarantula as a pet?
faq.a: Scholars generally discourage keeping dangerous creatures without legitimate reason. Ownership must not expose others to risk.

Do NOT self-medicate with antimalarials without diagnosis

islamic_ruling.status: Mu'dhī — The mosquito is mentioned in the Qur'an and is permissible to kill

hadith_primary.source: Qur'an 2:26 (Baqarah); scholars unanimously permit its killing

fiqh_ruling.shafii: Permissible; killing during prayer does not invalidate salah

fiqh_ruling.maliki: Permissible; prevention of its harm is obligatory where possible

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: Any swift method is permissible: swatting, electric racquet, sprays, repellents, nets. Fumigation is allowed.

duas.transliteration: A'udhu bi kalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq

Keep drains unblocked and flowing
physical_barriers: Install insect screens on all windows and doors
Use mosquito nets over beds, especially for children

Use DEET or picaridin-based repellents on exposed skin

Pyrethroid-based indoor sprays
Burning mosquito coils (outdoor or well-ventilated areas)
Larvicide (Bacillus thuringiensis israelensis) in standing water
Professional fogging for severe infestations
prohibited_methods: Setting fire to breeding areas (burning wetlands, fields) — prohibited for ecological harm
Any method causing mass non-target insect death unnecessarily

eschatology.qiyamah_note: The mosquito is cited by Allah in the Qur'an as a sign of His ability to illustrate truth through the smallest of creation. Even it is part of the divine design. Prevention and targeted killing is the Islamic approach.
eschatology.ecological_note: Mosquitoes are food for birds, bats, and fish. Targeted killing of adult mosquitoes in human areas is preferred over ecosystem-wide pesticide use.

climate_future.trend: Massive range expansion and longer breeding seasons across India due to warming
affected_regions: Previously malaria-free northern Indian hills now seeing endemic transmission
Dengue expanding from coastal cities to interior states
climate_future.projection: By 2050, an additional 1 billion people globally at risk from Aedes-borne diseases; India will bear disproportionate burden

faq.q: Can I kill a mosquito while performing Salah?
faq.a: Yes. Scholars of all four schools permit swatting a mosquito during prayer as a minor, necessary action that does not invalidate Salah.

faq.q: What is the most effective mosquito repellent?
faq.a: DEET (20–30%) is the gold standard. Natural alternatives include eucalyptus oil (PMD) and neem oil, effective for 2–3 hours.

faq.q: How do I stop mosquitoes from breeding in my home?
faq.a: Empty ALL standing water every 3–4 days. A mosquito can breed in as little as 5ml of water in a bottle cap.

faq.q: Is burning mosquito coils halal?
faq.a: Yes. Using coils to repel or kill mosquitoes is permissible. Ensure ventilation to avoid smoke inhalation by household members.

faq.q: Which mosquito carries malaria vs dengue?
faq.a: Anopheles (bites at night, grey body) carries malaria. Aedes aegypti (bites by day, black/white striped) carries dengue, Zika, and Chikungunya.

eschatology.qiyamah_note: All creatures will account to Allah. Kill only with necessity and swiftly. The cockroach, though repugnant, is part of the ecosystem that processes organic waste.
eschatology.ecological_note: In outdoor environments, cockroaches serve as decomposers. Only domestic cockroach species that contaminate human food need targeted elimination.

faq.q: Is killing cockroaches during Ramadan fasting permissible?
faq.a: Yes — dealing with harmful insects does not break the fast and is encouraged for home hygiene.

faq.q: Does seeing a cockroach mean my home is dirty?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Cockroaches can enter clean homes through grocery bags, cardboard, and drainage systems. A rapid response is more important than shame.

faq.q: What is the fastest way to eliminate cockroaches?
faq.a: Gel bait combined with boric acid is the most effective long-term strategy. Results within 1–2 weeks.

faq.q: Can cockroaches cause asthma?
faq.a: Yes. Cockroach allergen is a significant asthma and allergy trigger, especially for children. Elimination reduces asthma attacks.

faq.q: Are cockroach repellents sufficient?
faq.a: Repellents move cockroaches — they do not eliminate them. Baits and boric acid that cockroaches ingest are far more effective.

faq.q: What does the Hadith of the fly mean scientifically?
faq.a: Modern research has found that some flies carry antimicrobial peptides. Scholars note this is a miraculous sign; however, food contaminated by flies should generally be discarded for hygiene.

faq.q: How quickly does a fly contaminate food?
faq.a: Instantly — it regurgitates digestive juices and walks across food, transferring pathogens within seconds of landing.

faq.q: What is the best fly prevention strategy?
faq.a: Cover food always. Eliminate outdoor breeding sites (garbage, wet organic matter). Install window screens.

faq.q: Are electric fly killers (bug zappers) effective?
faq.a: UV fly killers are highly effective for flying insects. Place away from food preparation areas. Clean regularly.

faq.q: Is it permissible to use chemical fly sprays in the kitchen?
faq.a: Yes, but ensure food is covered and surfaces are wiped after spraying. Allow ventilation before returning to the area.

eschatology.qiyamah_note: Even the smallest of creatures will account to Allah. The flea that carries plague has caused millions of deaths in history — a reminder of how seemingly insignificant creation can be a vehicle of great trial.

faq.q: Can fleas jump from pets to humans?
faq.a: Yes — cat and dog fleas readily bite humans, especially on ankles and legs. Treat both the pet and the home simultaneously.

faq.q: How long can fleas survive without a host?
faq.a: Adult fleas can survive 1–2 weeks without feeding. Eggs in carpet can remain dormant for months.

faq.q: Is the Prophet's killing of a flea authentic?
faq.a: Yes — referenced in several Hadith collections, indicating permissibility of killing fleas even during Ihram.

faq.q: What is the fastest way to get rid of fleas?
faq.a: Treat pet + wash all bedding at 60°C + diatomaceous earth on carpets simultaneously. Repeat in 2 weeks for eggs.

faq.q: Can fleas transmit plague in India today?
faq.a: Bubonic plague is extremely rare in India now (last significant outbreak was Surat 1994). Surveillance continues in Himachal Pradesh. The risk is very low for urban populations.

faq.q: How do I properly remove a tick?
faq.a: Use fine tweezers, grasp as close to the skin as possible, and pull steadily and firmly outward. Do not twist or squeeze.

faq.q: What is CCHF and is it in India?
faq.a: Crimean-Congo Haemorrhagic Fever is a severe viral disease spread by Hyalomma ticks. Cases have been documented in Gujarat (2011 outbreak) and sporadic cases since.

faq.q: Can ticks live on my carpets?
faq.a: Brown dog ticks can complete their lifecycle indoors. Treat pets and vacuum/spray home if a pet brings ticks in.

faq.q: Is it permissible to kill ticks on my pet?
faq.a: Yes. Removing and killing ticks from pets is both permissible and a form of care for the animal under the Islamic principle of mercy to animals.

faq.q: What disease does a tick carry in India?
faq.a: Kyasanur Forest Disease in Karnataka/Goa; CCHF in Gujarat/Rajasthan; Tick Typhus in various states. Lyme disease is rare but documented.

eschatology.qiyamah_note: Even bed bugs, which cause great suffering and loss of sleep, are creatures of Allah's creation. Their elimination from our beds is a rightful protection of our health and rest.
eschatology.ecological_note: Bed bugs have no known ecological benefit outside their evolutionary role as parasites. Elimination from human habitation is wholly justified.

faq.q: Can I see bed bugs with the naked eye?
faq.a: Yes — adult bed bugs are about the size of an apple seed (4–5mm), reddish-brown, flat, and oval. Look in mattress seams, headboard crevices, and behind electrical sockets.

faq.q: How do bed bugs enter a home?
faq.a: Through second-hand furniture, luggage from infested hotels, and clothing. They do not fly and cannot jump — they walk and hitchhike.

faq.q: How long does it take to eliminate bed bugs?
faq.a: A single professional heat treatment can eliminate 100% of bed bugs in one session. Chemical treatment requires multiple visits over 2–4 weeks.

faq.q: Is it permissible to throw away an infested mattress?
faq.a: Yes. Discarding heavily infested items to protect household members is fully permissible and may be necessary.

faq.q: Do bed bugs transmit diseases?
faq.a: No confirmed disease transmission, but their bites cause severe allergic reactions in some people and chronic sleep deprivation, which has serious health consequences.

faq.q: Are glue traps permitted in Islam?
faq.a: Scholars generally discourage glue traps because animals caught on them suffer for extended periods, which violates the principle of Ihsan (excellence/mercy) in killing. Snap traps are preferred.

faq.q: Can rats chew through concrete?
faq.a: No — but they can chew through brick, wood, plastic, soft metal, and most materials. Seal with steel wool and concrete mortar.

faq.q: Why does the Hadith specifically mention the rat?
faq.a: The rat (fa'rah) was a direct household threat in 7th century Arabia, known for causing fires by gnawing on wicks, contaminating food stores, and biting sleeping people. The Prophet ﷺ explicitly named it.

faq.q: What diseases can I get from rat droppings?
faq.a: Hantavirus (dust from droppings), Leptospirosis (urine in water/food), Salmonella (food contamination). Always wear gloves and a mask when cleaning rat-infested areas.

faq.q: Is it permissible to use rat poison?
faq.a: First-generation rodenticides (warfarin) are permissible. Scholars caution against methods that cause prolonged suffering; ensure death is reasonably swift.

faq.q: How do I know if I have termites?
faq.a: Look for: mud tubes on walls, hollow-sounding wood, discarded wings near windows, sagging floors or ceilings, and tiny holes in drywall or wood.

faq.q: Can termites damage concrete or metal?
faq.a: No — but they penetrate through expansion joints and cracks to reach wood above concrete slabs. Metal structures are safe; any wood element is at risk.

faq.q: What is the termite story in the Qur'an?
faq.a: Qur'an 34:14 — When Prophet Sulayman ﷺ died while leaning on his staff, the jinn did not know he had passed until a termite consumed the staff and it broke. A profound sign of Allah's hidden knowledge.

faq.q: Is DIY termite control effective?
faq.a: For minor infestations, boric acid and bait stations can be helpful. For established colonies, professional treatment is strongly recommended.

faq.q: How long does professional termite treatment last?
faq.a: Soil termiticide barriers last 5–10 years. Bait station systems require annual monitoring. Annual inspections are essential in tropical India.

faq.q: Is it haram to kill ants?
faq.a: Passive, harmless ants should not be killed. Ants causing clear structural damage to property may be eliminated using targeted, non-burning methods.

faq.q: What is the Hadith about burning ant colonies?
faq.a: Sahih al-Bukhari 3319 — A prophet burned an ant colony after being stung once; Allah rebuked him for destroying an entire community for a single sting. This is a fundamental Islamic teaching on proportionality.

faq.q: How do I know if I have carpenter ants or termites?
faq.a: Carpenter ants leave smooth galleries (do not eat wood) and push out sawdust-like frass. Termites leave mud tubes and consume the wood itself.

faq.q: Can I use insecticide sprays on ants?
faq.a: For carpenter ants causing structural damage — yes. For harmless passing ants — it is better to discourage them with physical barriers (chalk lines, cinnamon, diatomite) than to spray.

faq.q: Do carpenter ants bite?
faq.a: Yes — they have powerful mandibles and can spray formic acid. The bite is painful but rarely causes serious medical problems in healthy adults.

permissible_methods: Professional bird netting installation
Trapping and humane relocation (specialist companies)
Avicidal gel (non-lethal sticky surface deterrent)
Falcon falconry deterrence (used at airports and large buildings in India)
prohibited_methods: Poisoning pigeons (causes slow death; also harms predators that eat the bodies)
Shooting with pellet guns in urban areas (unsafe, inhumane)

eschatology.qiyamah_note: The pigeon is a creature of Allah. The emphasis in Islamic law is on deterrence and relocation before lethal control. If we must kill, it must be swift, purposeful, and not wasteful.
eschatology.ecological_note: Urban pigeons are descendants of domesticated birds released by humans. They are a human-created problem requiring human solutions. Reducing food availability is the single most effective long-term strategy.

climate_future.trend: Urban pigeon populations continue to grow with expanding cities and food waste
affected_regions: All major Indian cities face growing pigeon nuisance
New construction creating more nesting ledges
climate_future.projection: Cities investing in integrated pigeon management (fertility control, deterrence, waste management) see significant long-term reduction

faq.q: Is it permissible to eat a pigeon caught as pest control?
faq.a: Yes — pigeons are halal to eat. If you must cull them, slaughtering them properly (zabiha) and consuming them is more virtuous than wasting the life.

faq.q: What is the fastest way to deter pigeons?
faq.a: Professional bird netting over the affected area is the most effective long-term deterrent. Spikes work on narrow ledges.

faq.q: Are pigeon droppings really dangerous?
faq.a: Yes — dried pigeon droppings contain Histoplasma fungus spores and Cryptococcus. Always wear a mask and gloves when cleaning. Wet the area first.

faq.q: Can I use poison pellets for pigeons?
faq.a: Poison is strongly discouraged — it causes slow, painful death (prohibited), and secondary poisoning of kites, hawks, and cats that eat the dead birds.

faq.q: Is feeding pigeons at mosques or dargahs permissible?
faq.a: Scholars generally permit it as kindness to animals, but if it creates a nuisance or health hazard to the community, the general harm principle (la darar) would suggest reducing it.

india_distribution: Pan-India — India has ~30 million stray dogs, the world's largest population

symptoms: Dog bite: deep puncture wounds, tearing injuries
Rabies: hydrophobia, aerophobia, paralysis, coma, death
health.fatality_india: ~20,000 rabies deaths/year — India accounts for 36% of global rabies deaths; 7.5 million dog bite cases/year
first_aid: Wash bite wound immediately with soap and water for 15 minutes (critical — reduces rabies risk significantly)
Apply antiseptic (povidone-iodine)
Go to hospital within hours for post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP)
PEP is FREE at government hospitals — 5 doses over 28 days
health.emergency_number: 108 (India Emergency); Anti-Rabies Clinics at all district hospitals

islamic_ruling.status: Al-Kalb al-'Aqūr — The biting dog: Explicitly named Fāsiq in Sahih al-Bukhari

hadith_primary.source: Sahih al-Bukhari 1829; Sahih Muslim 1198
hadith_primary.translation_en: Five animals are fāsiq (harmful) and may be killed even in the Haram: the crow, the kite, the scorpion, the rat, and the biting dog.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: Khamsun mina'd-dawabbi kulluhunna fasiqun yuqtalna fi'l-harami: al-ghurabu wa'l-hida'atu wa'l-aqrabu wa'l-fa'ratu wa'l-kalbu'l-aqur.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: The biting, aggressive, or rabid dog may be killed; a pet or guard dog that does not attack is not targeted
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Same distinction — only the mu'dhī (harmful) dog is targeted
fiqh_ruling.hanbali: Permissible to kill aggressive dogs in self-defence or community protection
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Permissible; the community's safety takes precedence

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: Self-defence killing is permissible when attacked. For community stray dog management, scholars recommend humane culling by authorities or ABC (Animal Birth Control) programmes as preferred approach.

duas.transliteration: A'udhu bi kalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
duas.translation_en: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.

prophetic_practice: Do not walk alone at night in areas known for stray dogs
Carry a stick when in high-risk areas
Avoid direct eye contact with an aggressive dog — look down and back away slowly
Do not run from a dog — it triggers the chase reflex

environmental: Eliminate open garbage (primary food source attracting strays)
Install secure garbage enclosures
Do not leave food scraps outside
Report stray dog packs to municipal animal control
physical_barriers: Secure compound walls
Install chain-link or solid gates
Use motion-sensor deterrent lights in compound areas at night
Train household members in safe dog encounter behaviour

permissible_methods: Self-defence: any necessary force when directly attacked
Report aggressive packs to municipal animal control for capture
Community-level Animal Birth Control (ABC) + rabies vaccination programme (recommended national strategy)
Professional dog catcher (municipal authority)
prohibited_methods: Poisoning stray dogs (slow, cruel death — prohibited; also risks poisoning other animals and humans)
Setting dogs against each other for entertainment

eschatology.qiyamah_note: Dogs are among Allah's creation. The Prophet ﷺ showed mercy to a woman who watered a thirsty dog and was forgiven by Allah. We must never be cruel. Killing is only permissible in genuine self-defence or community safety — not out of disgust.
eschatology.ecological_note: India's stray dog crisis is a human-created problem — the result of uncontrolled breeding, irresponsible ownership, and poor waste management. The solution must be systematic (ABC, vaccination, waste control) not individual culling.

climate_future.trend: Urban stray populations growing with expanding cities and waste streams
affected_regions: All Indian metropolitan areas
Peri-urban zones with poor waste management
climate_future.projection: Without sustained ABC + vaccination programmes, India's rabies deaths will continue. WHO targets rabies elimination by 2030 — India is far behind schedule.

faq.q: What should I do if bitten by a dog?
faq.a: 1. Wash the wound for 15 minutes with soap and water. 2. Apply iodine. 3. Go to the nearest government hospital within hours. 4. Start the 5-dose rabies PEP vaccine course. Do NOT miss doses.

faq.q: Are all stray dogs rabid?
faq.a: No — most are not rabid. But in India, you cannot know for certain. Always treat a bite as a potential rabies exposure and seek PEP.

faq.q: Is it permissible to keep a dog as a pet in Islam?
faq.a: Scholars permit keeping dogs for guarding property, livestock protection, and hunting. Keeping as a companion pet inside the home is generally discouraged by the majority of scholars due to purity concerns (najasah).

faq.q: What does the Kalb al-'Aqur (biting dog) ruling mean?
faq.a: It specifically refers to an aggressive, biting dog. A tame, non-aggressive dog is not classified as a Fāsiq creature and should not be harmed.

faq.q: What is the Anti-Rabies Vaccine situation in India?
faq.a: Post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP) — the 5-dose vaccine course — is free at all government hospitals in India. The key is starting it within 24–48 hours of exposure. It is 100% effective when given before symptoms appear.

latin_name: Pteropus giganteus (Indian Flying Fox) / Rhinolophus spp.

india_distribution: Pan-India; Indian Flying Fox widespread in peninsular India; insectivorous bats in all regions

diseases: Rabies (rare in Indian bats but possible)
symptoms: Bat bite: small puncture wound (often painless — check skin after bat contact)
health.fatality_india: Nipah outbreaks: Kerala 2018 (17 deaths), 2023 (2 deaths); case fatality rate 40–75%
first_aid: Any bat contact (bite or scratch, even minor): wash with soap and water and seek rabies PEP immediately
Do NOT handle bats with bare hands
Wear N95 mask when cleaning bat roosting areas
health.emergency_number: 108 (India Emergency); State Health Departments for Nipah monitoring

islamic_ruling.status: Mu'dhī when roosting in homes and posing disease risk — permissible to exclude and kill when necessary

hadith_primary.source: The Khuffash (bat) is classified as a harmful creature in classical fiqh due to its impurity (filth from guano) and disease risk
hadith_primary.translation_en: There shall be no harm and no reciprocating harm.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: La darara wa la dirar.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Permissible to exclude from living spaces; killing permissible if it poses a genuine disease risk
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Permissible when causing harm
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Permissible when the harm is established

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: Exclusion (one-way bat valves at dusk) is the preferred method — bats are released to fly away. Killing as a last resort must be swift.

duas.transliteration: A'udhu bi kalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
duas.translation_en: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created.

prophetic_practice: Seal roof gaps before bats roost
Avoid handling dead bats
Wear gloves when inspecting roof spaces

environmental: Seal all roof entry points before the bat roosting season (February–March)
Install fine mesh over roof vents and gaps
Remove standing water near the home (reduces insect food source)
physical_barriers: One-way exclusion devices during non-roosting hours
Caulk all gaps larger than 6mm in roof structures
Bright lighting in potential roosting areas (bats avoid lit spaces)

permissible_methods: Professional bat exclusion (one-way valve at entry points — bats exit but cannot return)
Exclusion must NOT be done June–August (maternity season — young bats cannot fly)
Seal all gaps after confirmed exclusion
prohibited_methods: Killing during maternity season (young will die inside the structure)
Sealing exit points before bats have left (they will die trapped inside)

eschatology.qiyamah_note: Bats are among the living communities of Allah. The Nipah virus they carry is a reminder of the delicate balance of creation. When we destroy bat habitats (deforestation), bats come closer to human settlements and spillover events increase.
eschatology.ecological_note: Insectivorous bats consume thousands of mosquitoes per night. A single bat eats up to 1,200 mosquitoes per hour. Their ecological value is immense. Exclusion rather than killing is strongly preferred.

climate_future.trend: Deforestation forcing bats into urban areas; spillover disease risk increasing
affected_regions: Kerala, Karnataka, and West Bengal are Nipah and bat-borne virus hotspots
Urban bat colonies expanding into new cities with construction creating new roost sites
climate_future.projection: Nipah virus spillover events expected to increase with forest fragmentation; prevention requires both exclusion and forest conservation

faq.q: If a bat has been in my room, do I need a rabies vaccine?
faq.a: If you found a bat in a room where you were sleeping, or had any direct contact, seek medical advice immediately for rabies PEP assessment. Bat bites can be so small they are unfelt.

faq.q: What is Nipah virus and should I be worried?
faq.a: Nipah is a serious bat-borne virus with 40–75% case fatality rate. It requires close contact with bat bodily fluids or infected humans to spread. Avoid consuming fruit bitten by bats (especially in Kerala season).

faq.q: Are all bats in India protected by law?
faq.a: Most Indian bat species are protected under the Wildlife Protection Act 1972. Killing bats is a legal offence. Exclusion is the legally and Islamically recommended approach.

faq.q: How do I know if bats are in my roof?
faq.a: Signs: oily stains at entry points, small brown droppings (like rice grains) in the attic, scratching sounds at dusk and dawn, musty ammonia smell.

faq.q: Can I do bat exclusion myself?
faq.a: For small colonies, yes — install one-way flaps at dusk entry points after observing where bats exit. For large colonies or unknown entry points, use a professional. Do not attempt in June–August.

biology.diet: Mosquitoes, cockroaches, moths, ants, other insects
india_distribution: Pan-India — extremely common in all Indian homes

symptoms: Droppings on walls and surfaces (aesthetic/hygiene concern)
health.fatality_india: Not considered medically dangerous in modern consensus
first_aid: Wash hands after handling
Clean droppings promptly

islamic_ruling.status: Al-Wazagha — The Prophet ﷺ encouraged killing it; one of the few creatures where specific reward is mentioned for killing

hadith_primary.source: Sahih Muslim 2240; Sunan Abu Dawud 5263
hadith_primary.translation_en: Whoever kills a gecko in the first strike, 100 good deeds are recorded for him; in the second strike, fewer than that; and in the third strike, fewer than that.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: Man qatala wazaghatan fi awwali darbatun kutibat lahu mi'atu hasanatin wa fi't-thaniyate duna dhalika wa fi't-thali-thate duna dhalika.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Strongly encouraged to kill; Prophetic narration is explicit and in major collections
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Encouraged; the Wazaghah blew on the fire of Ibrahim ﷺ according to Hadith — hence the encouragement to kill it
fiqh_ruling.hanbali: Encouraged; Hadith in Sahih Muslim is explicit
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Permissible and encouraged

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: Swift single strike. The Hadith rewards the first swift strike most — a reminder that even when killing is encouraged, it must be swift and not prolonged.

duas.transliteration: Bismillahir-rahmanir-rahim
duas.translation_en: Recite Bismillah and proceed swiftly — the reward is in the first strike.

prophetic_practice: Kill swiftly in a single strike if you choose to — the reward diminishes with each additional strike
Keep home clean to reduce the insect prey that attracts geckos

environmental: Reduce insects that attract geckos: seal gaps, use screens, reduce standing water (mosquitoes are their main food)
Turn off unnecessary outdoor lights at night (light attracts insects which attract geckos)
physical_barriers: Seal cracks around window frames
Apply gecko-repellent strips near entry points
Use peacock feather bundles near entry points (traditional Indian remedy — anecdotally effective)

permissible_methods: Direct strike with a rolled newspaper or slipper
Glue boards near walls (check frequently — release outdoors quickly if alive)
Commercial gecko repellent sprays (natural: phenyl, naphthalene balls)
Catch-and-release outdoors

eschatology.qiyamah_note: The gecko is unique — it is one of the few creatures where the Prophet ﷺ explicitly linked killing it to reward. The reason given in some narrations is that it blew on the fire used to burn Ibrahim ﷺ. Whether metaphorical or literal, it teaches us that harm to the prophets and their followers has consequences even in the smallest of creation.
eschatology.ecological_note: Geckos provide genuine pest control, especially of mosquitoes and cockroaches. The Prophetic encouragement to kill must be weighed against the practical benefit geckos provide in a home. Some scholars note the encouragement applies more specifically to the context of worship areas.

climate_future.trend: Stable or increasing urban gecko populations with climate warming and urban heat islands
affected_regions: Pan-India — all urban areas
New species expanding northward with warming winters
climate_future.projection: Gecko control will remain a constant feature of Indian domestic life indefinitely

faq.q: Why does the Prophet ﷺ encourage killing the gecko?
faq.a: Hadith narrations state that the wazaghah (gecko) blew on the fire built to burn Prophet Ibrahim ﷺ, thereby assisting in harming a Prophet of Allah. Whether interpreted literally or as a symbolic distinction, the ruling is explicit in Sahih Muslim.

faq.q: Is the house gecko really harmful?
faq.a: Medically, the common house gecko is not considered dangerous by modern science. The Islamic ruling on it is based on Prophetic tradition, not modern medical classification.

faq.q: Should I kill geckos if they eat mosquitoes?
faq.a: There is a practical dilemma here — geckos provide real pest control. Some scholars have noted the reward mentioned is for a specific type (the fan-footed wazaghah of the Arabian Peninsula). Use your judgement while acknowledging the Prophetic tradition.

faq.q: Is it permissible to keep a gecko as a pet?
faq.a: Scholars generally permit keeping non-harmful animals for legitimate purposes. Given the Prophetic encouragement to kill the wazaghah, keeping one as a pet would be viewed critically.

faq.q: What if I hit it twice — does the reward halve?
faq.a: According to the Hadith: yes, the reward in the second strike is less than the first, and less still in the third. This teaches us to be precise, swift, and not prolonged in necessary killing.

india_distribution: Extremely common during monsoon season across all states

health.fatality_india: Frogs cause zero human fatalities
first_aid: N/A — frogs are harmless to humans

islamic_ruling.status: Al-Difa' — Explicitly FORBIDDEN to kill by authentic Hadith

hadith_primary.source: Sunan Abu Dawud 5269; Sahih — Imam Ahmad confirms
hadith_primary.translation_en: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the killing of frogs.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: Naha rasulullahi sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallama 'an qatlihi.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Forbidden (haram) to kill; the Hadith is explicit
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Forbidden; scholars add that the frog's croaking is dhikr (glorification of Allah)
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Forbidden; additionally forbidden to eat

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: STRICTLY FORBIDDEN to kill. Relocate humanely if found inside the home. Open a door or window and allow it to exit.

prophetic_practice: If a frog enters the home during monsoon, gently guide it out
Respect the frog's natural habitat — do not drain ponds unnecessarily
The frog is said to glorify Allah with its croaking — a reminder of dhikr

environmental: Seal gaps at ground level to prevent frogs entering during monsoon
Install mesh at drainage entry points
physical_barriers: Threshold sweeps along ground-level doors during peak monsoon

permissible_methods: Gently catch in a container and release outdoors
Open doors/windows to allow it to exit on its own
prohibited_methods: Applying salt or chemicals to the skin (osmotic shock — causes painful death)

eschatology.qiyamah_note: The frog glorifies Allah with its voice — this is mentioned by some scholars as the reason for the prohibition. Every creature of Allah has its form of worship. We must not silence them without just cause.
eschatology.ecological_note: Frogs are apex predators of mosquito larvae. A single frog pond eliminates millions of mosquito breeding cycles. Their protection is not just Islamic — it is an ecological imperative.

climate_future.trend: Indian frog populations declining due to habitat loss, pesticide use, and collection for frog legs export
affected_regions: Wetland destruction in Punjab, Haryana, and Rajasthan devastating frog populations
Rice paddy pesticide use killing frog populations that naturally reduce mosquito breeding
climate_future.projection: Loss of frog populations directly correlated with increase in mosquito-borne diseases in agricultural India

faq.q: Why is killing a frog haram?
faq.a: Sunan Abu Dawud 5269 contains an explicit Prophetic prohibition on killing frogs. It is a clear Hadith (sahih) and all four schools of fiqh agree on the prohibition.

faq.q: What should I do if a frog enters my home?
faq.a: Gently guide it toward an open door using a broom or container. Do not harm it. In Islam, helping a creature to safety is an act of reward.

faq.q: Can I eat frog legs?
faq.a: No. The majority of Islamic scholars (Hanafi, Hanbali, Maliki) forbid eating frogs on the basis of the same Hadith prohibiting their killing. Shafi'i scholars have a minority view permitting it if slaughtered; however the dominant ruling is prohibition.

faq.q: Is the frog's croaking dhikr?
faq.a: Some classical scholars interpreted the frog's sound as a form of glorification of Allah (tasbih), which is one reason offered for the prohibition on killing them. This is mentioned by Imam al-Nawawi and others.

faq.q: What if a frog is in my kitchen contaminating food?
faq.a: Relocate it outside immediately. The fact that it is inconvenient does not make killing permissible. There is no exemption for killing frogs in domestic settings.

india_distribution: Pan-India; migratory populations from Central Asia visit in winter; resident populations in peninsular India

health.fatality_india: Zero
first_aid: N/A

islamic_ruling.status: Al-Hudhud — Honoured bird mentioned by name in the Qur'an (27:20); Forbidden to kill

hadith_primary.source: Sunan Abu Dawud 5267; authentic chain
hadith_primary.translation_en: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the killing of four creatures: the ant, the bee, the hoopoe, and the shrike.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: Naha rasulullahi sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallama 'an qatlihi.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Forbidden to kill
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Forbidden; the Hoopoe is named in the Qur'an as a noble creature
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Forbidden

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: STRICTLY FORBIDDEN. If found injured, contact the Forest Department of India for wildlife assistance.

surahs: An-Naml (27) — contains the story of the Hoopoe (Hudhud) and Prophet Sulayman ﷺ
prophetic_practice: Honour the hoopoe if you see one
Do not disturb its nesting
If found injured, assist in its recovery if possible

environmental: The hoopoe is not a pest — no prevention measures needed
physical_barriers: N/A

permissible_methods: Gentle relocation if found inside a home — open windows and allow it to exit
Contact State Forest Department if injured
prohibited_methods: Capturing for pet trade (illegal under Wildlife Protection Act 1972 in India)

eschatology.qiyamah_note: The Hoopoe was the messenger of Prophet Sulayman ﷺ, the one who brought news of the Queen of Sheba (Bilqis) and her people worshipping the sun. It is mentioned by name in Surat Al-Naml — a unique honour no other bird receives in the Qur'an.
eschatology.ecological_note: Hoopoes are insectivores that control soil insect populations. They nest in tree holes and are an important indicator of healthy agricultural landscapes.

climate_future.trend: Stable populations in India; potential disruption from agricultural intensification
affected_regions: Traditional wintering grounds in Rajasthan and Gujarat well-preserved
Threat from pesticide use in agricultural areas reducing prey availability
climate_future.projection: Long-term stability if agricultural practices reduce pesticide use in key hoopoe wintering areas

faq.q: Where is the Hoopoe mentioned in the Qur'an?
faq.a: Surah An-Naml 27:20-28 — Prophet Sulayman ﷺ noticed the Hudhud was absent from his assembly. The Hudhud later arrived with news of the Queen of Sheba and her people worshipping the sun instead of Allah.

faq.q: Why is the Hoopoe protected in Islam?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ explicitly forbade its killing (Abu Dawud 5267). Its mention in the Qur'an and its historical role as a messenger in the Sulaymanic tradition give it special honour.

faq.q: Is the Hoopoe protected by Indian law?
faq.a: Yes — all wild birds in India are protected under the Wildlife Protection Act 1972. Killing, capturing, or trading hoopoes is a criminal offence.

faq.q: What does the Hoopoe look like?
faq.a: It is unmistakable: a peach-orange body, black and white striped wings and tail, and a distinctive fan-shaped orange-black crest on its head. About the size of a large starling.

faq.q: What should I do if I find an injured Hoopoe?
faq.a: Contact your State Forest Department wildlife rescue line, or a local bird rescue centre. Do not keep it; it is protected wildlife.

latin_name: Apis cerana indica (Indian Honey Bee) / Apis dorsata (Giant Rock Bee)

india_distribution: Pan-India; Indian honey bee (Apis cerana indica) is the primary domestic species; giant rock bee in Himalayas, Western Ghats, Nilgiris

symptoms: Normal sting: pain, swelling, redness — resolves in hours
Anaphylaxis (rare): throat swelling, breathing difficulty — medical emergency
health.fatality_india: Rare anaphylactic deaths; mass attack (bee swarm) fatalities possible but uncommon
first_aid: Remove stinger by scraping (do NOT squeeze)
Apply cold compress
Antihistamine for mild reaction
Epinephrine (EpiPen) for anaphylaxis + call 108 immediately

islamic_ruling.status: Al-Nahlah — The bee is honoured in the Qur'an with an entire Surah named after it (Surah An-Nahl); FORBIDDEN to kill

hadith_primary.source: Sunan Abu Dawud 5267
hadith_primary.translation_en: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the killing of four creatures: the ant, the bee, the hoopoe, and the shrike.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: Naha rasulullahi sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallama 'an qatlihi.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Forbidden to kill; honey is halal and blessed
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Forbidden; the bee is honoured in Qur'an 16:68-69
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Forbidden; killing bees unnecessarily is a sin

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: FORBIDDEN to kill. If a hive is in an inconvenient location, contact a local beekeeper for FREE hive relocation — they will be happy to take the colony.

surahs: An-Nahl (16) — Surah of the Bee: 'And your Lord inspired the bee: Take your dwellings in the mountains, trees, and what they erect. Then eat from all the fruits.' [16:68–69]
prophetic_practice: Do not destroy bee hives unnecessarily
Consume honey — the Prophet ﷺ loved honey and recommended it as a cure
Contact beekeepers for hive relocation — never kill
If stung: be calm, remove the stinger by scraping

environmental: Seal wall cavities before spring to prevent swarms from establishing hives
Contact a local beekeeper if you notice bee scouts inspecting your property
physical_barriers: Seal gaps in eaves and roof soffits before February
Do not disturb bee swarms — a stationary swarm is resting and will move on within 24–48 hours

permissible_methods: Contact a local beekeeper for FREE hive relocation (they take the honey and the colony)
Wait for a swarm to move on naturally (swarms are temporary — 24–72 hours)
Bee smoker (non-lethal) to calm bees during relocation
prohibited_methods: Any method that kills the colony

eschatology.qiyamah_note: Allah revealed an entire Surah of the Qur'an named after the bee (An-Nahl). The bee is commanded by Allah to produce honey, which is described as a healing for people (Qur'an 16:69). Their creation is a sign (āyah) of Allah's wisdom.
eschatology.ecological_note: Bees pollinate 70% of the world's food crops. The collapse of bee populations is one of the greatest existential threats to human food security. Their protection is not only Islamic — it is a survival imperative for all of humanity.

climate_future.trend: Indian bee populations declining due to pesticide use, habitat loss, and climate disruption of flowering seasons
affected_regions: Monoculture farming regions in Punjab, Haryana see declining bee populations
Climate shifts disrupting synchronisation between bee activity and flowering seasons
climate_future.projection: Without aggressive bee conservation policies, Indian agricultural output could decline significantly; pollination services are valued at trillions globally

faq.q: What does the Qur'an say about bees?
faq.a: An entire Surah is named after the bee (An-Nahl, Chapter 16). Verses 68–69: Allah inspired the bee to build hives in mountains, trees, and structures, then eat of all fruits and produce honey — a healing for people.

faq.q: A swarm of bees has settled near my home — what should I do?
faq.a: Do not disturb them — swarms are temporary (24–72 hours) and will move on to find a permanent home. If they stay longer, call a local beekeeper. Never spray or burn.

faq.q: Is it permissible to consume honey from non-Muslim beekeepers?
faq.a: Yes. Honey is inherently halal regardless of who keeps the bees, as it is not an animal product requiring slaughter.

faq.q: What if a bee hive is inside the wall of my home?
faq.a: Contact a local beekeeper for free relocation. Do not spray insecticide into the wall — the honey will ferment and cause structural damage. Beekeepers will remove the colony and clean the cavity.

faq.q: Is it permissible to kill a bee in self-defence if it is stinging?
faq.a: Scholars generally permit defending oneself from an immediate sting, but this does not extend to destroying the colony. Swatting one bee that is actively stinging you is different from killing an entire hive.

india_distribution: Pan-India — ubiquitous in all Indian ecosystems and homes

diseases: Fire ants (Solenopsis invicta — invasive): painful mass stings
Pharaoh ants: mechanical pathogen carriers in hospitals
symptoms: Fire ant mass attack: raised pustules
Pharaoh ant contamination in hospital settings: risk of wound infection
health.fatality_india: No direct fatalities from common Indian ant species
first_aid: Cold compress for stings
Antihistamine for allergic reaction
Medical attention for fire ant mass attacks

islamic_ruling.status: Al-Namlah — Explicitly FORBIDDEN to kill in Hadith; and glorified in the Qur'an (Surah An-Naml)

hadith_primary.source: Sunan Abu Dawud 5267; Sahih al-Bukhari 3319
hadith_primary.translation_en: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the killing of four creatures: the ant, the bee, the hoopoe, and the shrike.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: Anna nabiyyan mina'l-anbiya'i qarasat-hu namlatun fa-amara bi-qaryati'n-namli fa-uhriqat fa-awhallahu ilayhi: An qarasat-ka namlatun ahraqta ummatan mina'l-umami tusabbih.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Forbidden to kill the common ant; harmful invasive species may be controlled with non-lethal methods
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Forbidden; Ants were mentioned honourably in the Qur'an (An-Naml 27:18–19)
fiqh_ruling.hanbali: Forbidden; exception for ants causing clear harm (but burning is never permitted)
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Forbidden

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: FORBIDDEN to kill the common passive ant. Use physical barriers (chalk, diatomite) and seal food sources. Carpenter ants causing structural damage — see separate entry.

surahs: An-Naml (27) — The ant spoke: 'O ants, enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by Solomon and his soldiers.' [27:18] — the ant glorified Allah and warned her community, showing wisdom
prophetic_practice: Use barriers (chalk, cinnamon, diatomaceous earth) to redirect ants rather than kill them
Seal food sources tightly
Clean up food spills immediately
Do not pour boiling water on ant colonies

environmental: Keep kitchen surfaces clean and dry
Store all food in airtight containers
Fix any water leaks (ants need moisture)
Seal cracks in walls and floors
physical_barriers: Apply a line of chalk or cinnamon at entry points (ants dislike crossing these)
Diatomaceous earth barrier along baseboards
Vinegar spray on ant trails (disrupts pheromone trail)
Boric acid gel bait for Pharaoh ants in hospital/hygiene settings only

permissible_methods: Divert and deter using barriers (chalk, cinnamon, diatomite)
Seal all food entry points permanently
Vinegar spray on ant trails to disrupt communication
For Pharaoh ants in hygiene-critical settings: targeted boric acid gel bait (kills colony via worker-queen transmission)
prohibited_methods: Burning ant colonies — EXPLICITLY FORBIDDEN by Prophetic Hadith (Bukhari 3319)
Any mass killing of passive non-harmful ants

eschatology.qiyamah_note: The ant colony that was almost trampled by Prophet Sulayman ﷺ shouted out and was heard and respected. The ant spoke! Ants glorify Allah, have complex communities, and are given wisdom. They remind us that even the smallest communities have worth before Allah.
eschatology.ecological_note: Ants are among the most ecologically important creatures on Earth: they aerate soil, distribute seeds, break down organic matter, and control other insect populations. A world without ants would collapse agriculturally within years.

climate_future.trend: Invasive fire ant (Solenopsis invicta) expanding range in Asia; climate warming accelerates spread
affected_regions: Fire ants already present in parts of China and Southeast Asia; expected to reach India within 1–2 decades
Indian native ants increasingly displaced by invasive species
climate_future.projection: Invasive ant species are a growing ecological and agricultural threat; this is distinct from the native ants protected in Islamic tradition

faq.q: Why are ants protected in Islam?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ explicitly forbade killing ants (Abu Dawud 5267). Additionally, the Qur'an honours ants in Surah An-Naml with a speaking ant showing wisdom and warning her community. And Bukhari 3319 recounts a prophet being rebuked for burning an ant colony.

faq.q: Can I kill ants in my kitchen?
faq.a: Passive, harmless ants should be deterred rather than killed. Use chalk lines, cinnamon, or diatomaceous earth. Seal food sources. Only Pharaoh ants in hygiene-critical settings (hospitals) may be controlled with boric acid bait.

faq.q: What is the story of the prophet who burned ants?
faq.a: Sahih al-Bukhari 3319: A prophet was stung by one ant and ordered the entire ant colony burned. Allah revealed to him: 'A single ant stung you, and you burned an entire community that glorifies Allah?' — a foundational teaching on proportionality.

faq.q: Are fire ants the same as regular ants in terms of Islamic ruling?
faq.a: Fire ants (Solenopsis invicta) are an invasive species causing serious harm — scholars would apply the mu'dhī principle allowing targeted control when they are causing injury. The protection applies primarily to native, non-aggressive ant species.

faq.q: Can I use ant poison?
faq.a: For non-harmful ants — no. For harmful invasive ants or Pharaoh ants in hospital settings — targeted boric acid gel bait (not burning, not mass killing) may be permitted by scholars applying the harm principle.

islamic_ruling.status: Honoured — The spider spun a web over the cave of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ during the Hijra; strongly discouraged to kill

hadith_primary.source: The story of the spider's web at the cave of Thawr is referenced in Qur'an 9:40 and sirah literature
hadith_primary.translation_en: The spider web at the cave of Thawr was part of Allah's protection of His Prophet ﷺ during the Hijra — by Allah's will, the Quraysh turned away, seeing the unbroken web as evidence that no one had entered.
hadith_primary.transliteration_arabic: Naha rasulullahi sallallahu 'alayhi wa sallama 'an qatlihi.

fiqh_ruling.hanafi: Strongly discouraged to kill the non-venomous house spider; the cave story creates a special status
fiqh_ruling.shafii: Discouraged; some scholars classify it as protected due to its role in the Hijra narrative
fiqh_ruling.hanbali: Discouraged; it is a beneficial predator of harmful insects
fiqh_ruling.maliki: Discouraged

islamic_ruling.killing_method_notes: Do not kill common house spiders. They eat mosquitoes, flies, and cockroaches. If a web is unsightly, gently relocate the spider outside rather than kill it.

surahs: Al-'Ankabut (29) — Surah of the Spider: 'The example of those who take allies other than Allah is like that of the spider who takes a home.' [29:41] — a metaphor of fragility and dependency
prophetic_practice: Allow house spiders to remain — they perform pest control
If a spider is in an unwanted location, catch in a glass and release outdoors
Recite the story of the Hijra to children as a reminder of Allah's protection through even the smallest of creation

environmental: N/A — house spiders are welcome and beneficial
physical_barriers: If you wish to discourage spiders in specific areas: use cedar oil (natural deterrent)
Remove webs from high-traffic areas and spiders will relocate

permissible_methods: Gently catch in a glass and release outdoors
Remove unsightly webs with a broom — spider will relocate
Cedar oil spray as natural deterrent in specific areas

eschatology.qiyamah_note: The spider that spun its web over the cave of Thawr on the night of the Hijra served Allah's Prophet ﷺ in the most critical moment of his life. By Allah's permission, a tiny creature with a fragile home became a shield for the final Messenger. This is the greatest honour any creature could receive.
eschatology.ecological_note: House spiders consume thousands of mosquitoes, flies, and other insects per year. A home with spiders has significantly fewer harmful insects. They are silent, free pest controllers.

climate_future.trend: Stable; house spider populations self-regulate based on insect prey availability
affected_regions: Pan-India — common house spiders adapt to all environments
climate_future.projection: As mosquito and fly populations grow with climate change, spider populations will respond naturally — providing free, natural pest control

faq.q: What is the story of the spider at the cave of Thawr?
faq.a: During the Hijra (migration), the Prophet ﷺ and Abu Bakr ؓ hid in the cave of Thawr. A spider spun a web across the entrance overnight. When the Quraysh arrived, they saw the unbroken web and believed no one had entered, and turned away. By Allah's will, the spider's web became the Prophet's protection.

faq.q: Is it permissible to kill a house spider?
faq.a: Most scholars strongly discourage killing non-venomous house spiders due to their beneficial nature and the honour associated with them in the Hijra narrative. Relocate rather than kill.

faq.q: Is the Surah named after the spider?
faq.a: Yes — Surah Al-'Ankabut (Chapter 29) uses the spider's home as a metaphor for the fragility of false beliefs. The spider appears twice prominently in Islamic tradition.

faq.q: Do house spiders really eat mosquitoes?
faq.a: Yes — common house spiders, particularly daddy-long-legs (Pholcus phalangioides), actively hunt and consume mosquitoes, flies, and even other spiders. They are one of the most effective natural mosquito predators in homes.

faq.q: What if I am afraid of spiders (arachnophobia)?
faq.a: Arachnophobia is understood; however, Islam teaches us to manage our fears rather than harm creatures unnecessarily. Gently capture and release outdoors, or ask someone else to do it. Never kill due to fear alone.
